name: Mu Shu Vegetables
recipeIngredient: a few wood ear or cloud ear mushrooms, soaked in hot water for 30 minutes and then cut into thin strips
1 large onion, cut into very thin wedges
1 small head green cabbage, finely shredded
4 oz canned mushrooms, drained (or equivalent fresh, sauteed in water)
Soy sauce or tamari to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. Thaw the mu shu pancakes.
2. In a large wok or skillet, sauté the garlic and onions for three minutes in a little water or vegetable broth.
3. Add the cabbage, carrot, and mushrooms, and sauté until the cabbage just wilts.
4. Add the green onions, soy sauce (about 1-2 tablespoons), and about 1 tablespoon of hosin sauce. Heat through.
5. Place one pancake in the microwave for 10 seconds to make it more pliable.
6. Remove the heated pancake and spread a tablespoon of hosin sauce on it.
7. Spread about half a cup of the mu shu mixture onto the pancake.
8. Fold the bottom of the pancake up about one-quarter of the way, then fold in the two sides.
9. Roll the pancake to make it easier to eat.
10. You can add julienne strips of tofu or other vegetables you like (e.g., bean sprouts, celery, water chestnuts, bamboo shoots) before folding the pancake.
11. This dish is even better the second day. Do not put the ingredients in the pancakes until right before eating.
